hero.title: Dedicated Seva to Warkaris at Pandharpur Wari
hero.subtitle: National Service Scheme (NSS) volunteers supporting and assisting pilgrims, healthcare teams, and emergency services throughout the holy pilgrimage route.

about.title: About Pandharpur Wari & NSS Seva
about.desc_1: Pandharpur Ashadhi Wari is one of India's oldest and most revered annual pilgrimages, where millions of devotees (Warkaris) walk hundreds of kilometers to the holy town of Pandharpur.
about.desc_2: The National Service Scheme (NSS) volunteers play a vital role in ensuring a safe and smooth journey for pilgrims. From medical aid to crowd management and cleanliness drives, our volunteers serve with dedication and devotion.

services.title: Our Key Services
services.emergency_help_desc: Round-the-clock emergency assistance and support for pilgrims in distress.
services.missing_persons_desc: Helping reunite lost family members and pilgrims with their groups.
services.lost_found_desc: Reporting and recovering lost belongings along the pilgrimage route.
services.medical_assistance_desc: First-aid camps, medicine distribution, and coordination with ambulances.
services.volunteer_support_desc: Coordinating NSS volunteer deployment and support operations at key points.
services.info_center_desc: Providing route maps, schedules, dynamic halts, and official updates.
services.food_water_desc: Coordinating free meal distributions (Annadan) and clean drinking water facilities.
services.rest_area_desc: Guiding pilgrims to nearby Dharamshalas, tents, and dynamic resting halts.
services.traffic_updates_desc: Real-time updates on road closures, traffic diversions, and pedestrian paths.
services.police_assistance_desc: Coordinating with local police forces to maintain law, order, and security.

cta.title: Ready to Serve? Join Us as an NSS Volunteer
cta.subtitle: Make a difference in the lives of millions of pilgrims. Register now to join our support network and get deployed.

footer.desc: Empowering volunteers to assist pilgrims and support local administration during the holy Pandharpur Wari pilgrimage.
footer.copyright: © 2026 Pandharpur Wari NSS Seva Portal. All rights reserved.

about_page.subtitle: Discover the heritage of the pilgrimage and our volunteer missions
about_page.history_desc_1: The Pandharpur Wari is an 800-year-old pilgrimage tradition in Maharashtra. Pilgrims, known as Warkaris, travel to the Vithoba Temple in Pandharpur, singing devotional songs and carrying palanquins (Palkhis) of saints, notably Sant Dnyaneshwar and Sant Tukaram.
about_page.history_desc_2: The pilgrimage covers hundreds of kilometers on foot over 21 days, embodying equality, devotion, and community brotherhood, transcending all barriers of caste and status.
about_page.mission_desc: The National Service Scheme (NSS) aims to develop student volunteers' character through community service. At the Wari, our mission is to provide selfless aid, ensure safety, and improve facilities for the millions of walking pilgrims, adhering strictly to the motto 'Not Me But You'.
about_page.vision_desc: To ensure a safe, clean, and spiritually uplifting pilgrimage experience for every Warkari through structured volunteer support and seamless coordination with local authorities.
about_page.obj_1: Deploy volunteers at crucial transit and resting points along the Palkhi route.
about_page.obj_2: Deliver immediate first-aid, hydration, and medical guidance to weary pilgrims.
about_page.obj_3: Assist local law enforcement in crowd management, traffic control, and safety drives.
about_page.obj_4: Promote clean and green initiatives (Swachh Bharat) through active cleanliness drives.
about_page.volunteer_importance_desc: With millions of pilgrims on the move, public administrative systems face massive challenges. Volunteers act as the lifeblood of the event, offering immediate human assistance, guiding lost pilgrims, managing massive crowds, and ensuring that emergency medical care is accessible.
about_page.timeline_title: Major Milestones & Halts of the Wari
about_page.medical_help_desc: Providing basic first aid, organizing health camps, and helping severe cases access ambulance systems.
about_page.crowd_management_desc: Assisting police in queuing, barricading, and managing heavy movement at major temple entries and halts.
about_page.lost_found_desc: Running public announcement systems to quickly reunite family members and secure misplaced bags.
about_page.emergency_support_desc: Active 24/7 volunteer hotlines linked with district control rooms for rapid action during incidents.

alandi.title: Alandi / Dehu Departure
alandi.desc: The sacred journey begins as the Palkhis of Sant Dnyaneshwar (from Alandi) and Sant Tukaram (from Dehu) set off towards Pune.

pune.title: Pune Stay
pune.desc: Both Palkhis arrive in Pune city, creating a grand assembly where volunteers handle massive city traffic and crowd guidance.

saswad.title: Saswad Crossing
saswad.desc: The route traverses the scenic and challenging Dive Ghat, demanding active medical camps and hydration stands from volunteers.

jejuri.title: Jejuri Halt
jejuri.desc: The holy caravan reaches Jejuri, the town of Lord Khandoba. Massive gatherings require heavy crowd management.

lonand.title: Lonand to Phaltan
lonand.desc: Entering the rural heartlands, where volunteers assist in setting up camp areas, sanitation facilities, and clean drinking water.

velapur.title: Velapur Ringan
velapur.desc: The famous 'Ringan' events take place here, where horses run in circles. Volunteers help manage safe barriers for spectators.

wakhari.title: Wakhari Junction
wakhari.desc: All Palkhis converge in Wakhari, just a few kilometers outside Pandharpur. The excitement builds up under close security.

pandharpur.title: Pandharpur Arrival
pandharpur.desc: The final holy destination is reached on Ashadhi Ekadashi. Volunteers assist at Chandrabhaga river banks and temple queues.

services_page.title: Our Services & Assistance
services_page.subtitle: We provide a wide range of free assistance services to ensure the safety, health, and comfort of all pilgrims throughout the pilgrimage.

emergency_page.title: Emergency Helplines & Contacts
emergency_page.subtitle: Immediate emergency numbers and contacts of key personnel, medical services, and safety teams.

police.title: Police Department
police.desc: Local police station and security guards deployed along the route for law and order.

ambulance.title: Ambulance Seva
ambulance.desc: 24/7 emergency medical transport system for quick hospitalization.

medical.title: NSS Medical Camp
medical.desc: Main medical camp coordinate center for generic issues, pills, and basic aid.

fire.desc: Emergency fire control and rescue teams deployed at major halting camps.

women.title: Women Helpdesk
women.desc: Dedicated support and security guidance for female pilgrims and children.

child.title: Child Helpline
child.desc: Safety and immediate tracking of lost or unaccompanied children during the wari.

control.title: Wari Control Room
control.desc: District Administration centralized command center for emergency operations.

gallery_page.title: Wari Seva Gallery
gallery_page.subtitle: Moments of service, devotion, and harmony captured during the Pandharpur Wari pilgrimage.
gallery_page.caption: Devotion in action - NSS volunteers assisting along the route.

faq_page.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq_page.subtitle: Find quick answers to common queries regarding the Wari pilgrimage, NSS duties, and support facilities.
faq_page.a1: Pandharpur Wari is an annual pilgrimage to Pandharpur, Maharashtra, in honor of Deity Vithoba. Pilgrims carry Palkhis (palanquins) of saints and walk over 200 kilometers from Alandi/Dehu to Pandharpur, celebrating devotion, music, and brotherhood.
faq_page.q2: How can I volunteer for NSS Seva?
faq_page.a2: College students registered with NSS units can apply through their respective NSS Program Officers. Public volunteers can register through our upcoming online volunteer registration form under Phase 3 of this portal.
faq_page.q3: How do I report a missing person?
faq_page.a3: You can report a missing person at any nearest NSS Seva camp, local police helpdesk, or submit details online via the 'Report Missing' page. Our volunteers use PA systems and coordinate with police to locate them.
faq_page.q4: How do I contact emergency services?
faq_page.a4: Call the centralized emergency numbers (112 or 108) or refer to the 'Emergency Contacts' page on this portal for direct contact numbers of camp doctors, police helplines, and NSS coordinators.
faq_page.a5: This portal is managed by the National Service Scheme (NSS) coordination committee in collaboration with the District Administration and local volunteers to facilitate coordination during the wari.
faq_page.q6: How can I join the NSS?
faq_page.a6: You can join the NSS through your high school, college, or university NSS unit. Contact your college NSS Program Officer or administrative department to enroll as an official volunteer.
